content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN nodes and KKK leaf nodes, **rooted** at node 111.

KKK friends are stationed at node 111, each aspiring to reach a **distinct** leaf node.

Meanwhile, Chef can choose any one of the leaf nodes and start from that.

Every second:


Firstly, each friend will move to one of the children of their current node in order to reach a distinct leaf node.
Once the friends have moved, Chef can move to an adjacent node or choose to remain stationary.
When Chef and any group of friends occupy the same node, those friends are considered tagged by Chef.

If multiple friends are present at the same node as Chef simultaneously, all of them are considered tagged.

Chef **cannot** tag the same person twice. Additionally, once a friend reaches a leaf node, they cannot be tagged.

Find the **maximum** number of friends Chef can tag if he moves optimally.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case will contain an integer NNN, the number of nodes in the tree.
The next (N−1)(N - 1)(N−1) lines will describe the given tree. Each line contains two integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​ denoting an edge between node uiu_iui​ and node viv_ivi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** number of friends Chef can tag if he moves optimally.
constraints: It is guaranteed that the edges denote a tree.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef will start from node 444. After the first second:

All three friends would move to node 222.
Chef moves to node 222 and tags all 333 friends.
